evals.prompt: I'm building a new Axum web app and I want to add authentication. I need email/password login, passkeys, and MFA. My database is at postgres://myapp:secret@db.internal:5432/myapp. Can you set up yauth for me? I want the auth routes at /api/auth and I need a protected /api/me endpoint that returns the current user.
evals.expected_output: Complete Rust code with Cargo.toml dependencies, pool creation, migration running, YAuthBuilder with email-password + passkey + mfa plugins configured, router with /api/auth nest and protected /api/me endpoint using auth middleware
expectations: Cargo.toml includes yauth with email-password, passkey, and mfa features
Code creates a database pool and runs migrations
YAuthBuilder is configured with EmailPasswordConfig, PasskeyConfig, and MfaConfig
A protected /api/me route uses auth_middleware and extracts Extension<AuthUser>
YAuthConfig includes base_url, session settings, and trusted_origins

evals.prompt: I have a Vue 3 app (Vite + Vue Router) and I just added yauth to my backend. I need a login page, register page, and a dashboard that shows the user's email and role. The auth API is at /api/auth. Also need to handle the MFA challenge flow if the user has MFA enabled.
evals.expected_output: Vue 3 setup with YAuthPlugin installed, LoginPage with MFA challenge handling, RegisterPage, and DashboardPage using useSession composable, plus vue-router configuration
expectations: LoginPage uses LoginForm component with onMfaRequired handler
MfaChallenge component is shown when MFA is required during login
DashboardPage uses useSession or useYAuth to display user info
Vue Router is configured with routes for login, register, and dashboard

evals.prompt: My Axum app already uses utoipa to generate an openapi.json and orval to generate a TypeScript client from it. I want to add yauth for authentication but I don't want to break my existing client generation pipeline. How should I set this up? I also need my app's API calls to automatically carry the session cookie.
evals.expected_output: Explanation of keeping specs separate, using @yackey-labs/yauth-client alongside the app's own orval-generated client, and ensuring credentials: include for session cookies
expectations: Recommends keeping yauth's OpenAPI spec separate from the app's spec
Shows how to use @yackey-labs/yauth-client alongside the app's orval-generated client
Explains that credentials: include on fetch requests automatically carries the session cookie
Does NOT suggest merging yauth routes into the app's openapi.json
Mentions that both clients coexist since yauth routes are under /api/auth
